includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive database of contamination removal parameters, safety data, and laser cleaning specifications

contaminant.name: Anti-Seize Compound
contaminant.description: During assembly, anti-seize contamination forms as sticky metallic and graphite-based deposits so tenacious adhesion builds up over time from heat and friction on surfaces. This contamination exhibits heat-resistant properties and strong bonding on steel, stainless steel, and aluminum, which distinguishes it from rust or oils because mechanical removal damages substrates during process. Key challenges occur in removal due to that bonding, so laser cleaning ablates layers precisely and base materials remain unharmed after treatment.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Nickel compounds (as Ni) generation detected - low toxicity risk
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation. WARNING: Nickel compounds (as Ni) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
